text: The video shows the surgeon navigating previous incision sites between the larynx and the frontal abdominal wall, near the stomach. Using a surgical device, adhesions are removed to expose the genital organs, ensuring no bleeding occurs. The surgeon then examines the right fallopian tube, noting its normal appearance.

The surgeon identifies the Faleb Al-Yusra channel, noting a visible load extending along its length. The location for incision is determined, ensuring the knife remains away from the artery supplying the adjacent white tissue. The surgeon proceeds to cut parallel to the Faleb channel, removing it completely.
The surgeon washes the pelvic area to ensure there is no bleeding at the surgical site. A sample is placed inside a plastic bag using the left valve channel and removed from the abdominal cavity.
